value_chain_position: Diversified defense and aerospace prime across jet engines (Pratt & Whitney), missiles/radar/EW (Raytheon), and avionics (Collins Aerospace); sells systems to DoD, allied governments, and commercial airlines.
relative_comps: RTX is a mature prime — revenue CAGR of 10% and FCF margin of 9% reflect a mix of high-margin Raytheon munitions/missiles (defense_tech alignment) and the drag from Pratt GTF fleet-quality issues that consumed cash. Patriot/NASAMS missile demand is structurally elevated by NATO rearmament. However, the bulk of RTX is a legacy systems integrator with modest growth versus pure-play defense-tech disruptors like KTOS or AVAV. Theme fit is real but the s-curve is maturing — it belongs in the value lens more than the disruption lens.
